data_source: FY2007 annual report / 10-K, GE Sep-Oct 2008 guidance and Buffett deal filings

g4_revenue_trend.description: Strong industrial and infrastructure growth pre-crisis; 3Q08 guide cut driven by GE Capital stress, not industrial demand collapse

g5_gross_margin_trend.description: Industrial margins and returns remained solid pre-crisis; pressure came from financial services, not a two-year industrial margin collapse

g7_moat.description: The moat was not in GE Capital. It was in GE's industrial installed base, service backlogs, global infrastructure franchises, and quasi-systemic importance. Buffett was effectively underwriting whether the industrial crown jewels and government importance made GE solvent enough for a 10% preferred plus warrants deal.

control_groups.conclusion: PASS — needed a government rescue and was not a safe private capital candidate.
PASS — funding model was too fragile and the franchise was not strong enough to support Buffett-style rescue capital.

buffett_action: $3B in 10% perpetual preferred, callable after 3 years at a 10% premium, plus warrants for $3B of common at $22.25
actual_result: GE redeemed the preferred in 2011 at a 10% premium after Berkshire collected $900M of dividends; Berkshire later exercised the warrants cashlessly for 10.7M GE shares. Positive outcome, but smaller than GS/BAC.
key_insight: Crisis preferred channel: the common stock was messy, but the rescue-capital terms were excellent and GE remained solvent enough for Buffett to earn equity-like upside with bond-like protection.
